context: Tests whether the agent creates a well-structured skill following the write-a-skill conventions: correct directory layout, a SKILL.md with frontmatter and required sections, a properly-formatted description, and a review checklist that is actually applied.

checklist.name: Third-person description
checklist.description: The SKILL.md frontmatter description is written in third person (does NOT use 'I', 'me', 'my', 'we')

checklist.name: Use when triggers
checklist.description: The description includes a 'Use when' clause listing specific triggers (keywords, contexts, or situations)

checklist.name: Description length
checklist.description: The description field is 1024 characters or fewer

checklist.name: SKILL.md frontmatter
checklist.description: SKILL.md has YAML frontmatter with both 'name' and 'description' fields

checklist.name: Quick start section
checklist.description: SKILL.md contains a 'Quick start' section (or equivalent minimal-example section)

checklist.name: Workflows section
checklist.description: SKILL.md contains a 'Workflows' section with step-by-step processes

checklist.name: No time-sensitive info
checklist.description: SKILL.md does NOT contain time-sensitive information (specific dates, version numbers that change, current pricing, etc.)

checklist.name: Concrete examples
checklist.description: SKILL.md includes at least one concrete example (a sample post, a worked scenario, or an example output)

checklist.name: Consistent terminology
checklist.description: SKILL.md uses consistent terminology throughout (does not alternate between different names for the same concept)

checklist.name: Correct directory name
checklist.description: The skill directory is named 'social-media-scheduler' (or a kebab-case variant)

checklist.name: SKILL.md is only required file
checklist.description: The directory contains SKILL.md; any additional files (REFERENCE.md, EXAMPLES.md, scripts/) are optional and only present if the content genuinely warrants them
